question_text: On Earth, the gravitational field strength is approximately 10 N/kg. What does this mean?

options.label: A
options.text: A 1 kg mass experiences a gravitational force of 10 N

options.label: B
options.text: A 1 kg mass weighs 1/10 of a newton

options.label: C
options.text: Gravity pulls objects downwards at 10 metres per second

explanation: A gravitational field strength of 10 N/kg means that every kilogram of mass experiences a gravitational force of 10 newtons. This is the definition of field strength: force per unit mass.
